trivia_bullets.text: Suspicious Detonation is a red Sorcery from Murders at Karlov Manor — a murder-mystery set on Ravnica. The flavor text's observation ("Clearly someone wanted us to find this") positions the detonation as a clue planted by the murderer, consistent with the set's whodunit narrative structure [Scryfall].

The card costs 4R normally, but reduces by 3 if an artifact was sacrificed this turn — making it functionally a 1R spell in artifact-sacrifice builds. It also cannot be countered (including by ward). The uncounterability clause is unusual for a common sorcery and significantly improves its value in combo-protection contexts [Scryfall oracle text].

Flavor text: "Clearly someone wanted us to find this." — Runubi of the Foundway Associates [Scryfall]. Runubi is one of at least four named Foundway Associates members referenced across MKM flavor texts (also: Udol on Gearbane Orangutan, Imel on Offender at Large, Algrom on Torch the Witness). The Associates function as a secondary investigator ensemble distinct from R.A.M.I. [Scryfall, WebSearch: MTG Salvation MKM flavor text compilation].
sources: WebSearch: MTG Salvation MKM lore thread

trivia_bullets.text: Artist Joe Slucher. EDHREC rank 22414 [Scryfall]. The spiral-shell mechanical device in the art has no named in-world object; it reads generically as an Izzet-style arcane explosive rather than a canonically identified construct [Scryfall — no lore note].
